heaviest_rainfall.status: 37.41mm of rain was recorded as having fallen today, marking an exceptional precipitation event and it ranked top out of our records.  A rate of 2.2mm per hour was recorded during the 113.1 hour long storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 27.0mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Ranked highest for lengthiest precipitation event, today's storm spanned 113.1 hours and was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 2.2mm per hour.  37.41mm of rain fell in total.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning strikes in one day since 11 April 2025 is 23 and it happened on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm around.  It's the first significant stormy weather event since 08 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The ongoing rainy stretch, 8 days and counting started on 07 November 2025 and ranks 9th in length since records started in 23 September 2024.  The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and this has only occurred 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (185°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: The largest wind run ever was on 21 October 2012 with a value of 1737 miles.

speed.status: The largest wind speed ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 5%) and rainfall (up 0.1 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 351 m) and rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in light levels (up 2613 lux), cloud levels (up 439 m), and rainfall (down 0.1 mm) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 351.4m lower than yesterday, 231.2m lower than the past week, 439.3m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, cloud levels hit a low of 637m and a high of 1658m, averaging 602.0m; the past week and month reported somewhat higher averages.
cloud levels.summary: Cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; mild and pleasant. Also, neither too dry nor too humid and little or no wind. A few light raindrops may fall, cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions, and overcast conditions will limit sunlight. The weather may change rapidly and become worse.
